description: next/ v2.0 — Level-3 MOM OperationsResponse = the manufacturing actuals (B2MML OperationsPerformance). Opposite side to the OperationsRequest (ProductionOrder = PLAN/target). v2.0: the actuals per ProcessSegment are modeled out as a dedicated SMProfile-SegmentResponse (HAS_SEGMENT_RESPONSE, corresponds_to SegmentRequirement); measured actual fields carry the SDC quality facet. Plan-vs-actual + cross-domain deviations are Cross-Source (it-evaluator), not a single-entity constraints block.

attributes.name: production_order_no
attributes.description: Join key — the OperationsRequest whose actuals this confirms (RESPONDS_TO).

attributes.name: quantity_produced
attributes.description: Piece count actually produced (actual). Counterpart to qty_planned.

attributes.name: cycle_time_actual_sec
attributes.description: Actually measured cycle time per piece (s, actual).

attributes.name: actual_end_time
attributes.description: Actual completion time (actual). Counterpart to planned_end_time.

attributes.name: status
attributes.description: Actual status of the confirmation. MEASURED reality (2026-07-12): the bound source /api/confirmations carries NO status column at all, so sources/rest/erp-operations-response.json binds this attribute to the CONSTANT 'fertig' ({ const: 'fertig' }). 'fertig' is therefore the ONLY value any OperationsResponse can ever carry — this attribute is a constant, not a discriminator, and it MUST NOT be used as a `when` guard operand (a guard over a constant is a tautology; see cross-constraints/qty_shortfall.json, which is retired for exactly that defect, and ci/lint-cross-constraints.mjs, which now fails the build on it). The previous description claimed 'offen (open) … storniert (cancelled)' — pure fiction, no source ever produced either value. If a real ERP ever delivers a genuine confirmation status column, EXTEND this enum against a measurement (CLAUDE.md law 3) — do not improvise it.

attributes.name: completed_at
attributes.description: Completion timestamp of the confirmation, if set.

attributes.name: updated_at
attributes.description: Last change. Server-managed and volatile on every read (sim-v5 returns a fresh value per GET), so promotion:never — kept in the snapshot but excluded from the change-trigger and never published on its own.

relationships.description: v2.0 — Inverse of SegmentResponse.PART_OF: the actuals per ProcessSegment.

isa95.note: Level-3 MOM OperationsResponse (B2MML) = the actuals. v2.0: the actuals per ProcessSegment are modeled out as a dedicated SMProfile-SegmentResponse (HAS_SEGMENT_RESPONSE); article_ref/machine_ref/quantity_produced remain a denormalized header shortcut, while material consumed/produced per segment lives as MaterialActual (MaterialLot) in the SegmentResponses. Plan-vs-actual = Cross-Source reconcile. SPEC-VS-ACTUAL: measured actual product property values (process-specific, e.g. line_speed/thickness/area_weight) hang off the Response (or SegmentResponse, delivery telemetry) and are checked against the ProductDefinition recipe bands. Process-specific measurement attributes live in customer profiles/examples, not in the neutral profile.
